title: An Advanced Elective
prose: N.E.W.T.-level classes are smaller and stranger than anything before them — a handful of committed students clustered around a subject dense enough that the professor stops bothering with the basics almost immediately, assuming you've already done the reading.
It's harder than anything you've taken before, and more satisfying for it. There's a particular pleasure in a subject that finally trusts you to keep up.

choices.label: You're thriving under the harder pace.
